falsifiers.name: F1_sc_window_exists
falsifiers.desc: PASS = a finite interaction window exists where SC is the LEADING instability (no particle-hole order pre-empts it). FAIL = competing order always wins -> +@ dead.

falsifiers.name: F2_sc_leads_competitor
falsifiers.desc: PASS = SC crosses its Stoner threshold BEFORE the leading CDW/SDW/PS competitor. FAIL = a density-wave / phase-separation order is the first instability.

falsifiers.name: F3_roomt_relaxed_inside
falsifiers.desc: PASS = the relaxed room-T glue demand (349 meV mapped to U) lands inside the SC-leading window. FAIL = the coupling we NEED for room-T SC drives competing order.

falsifiers.name: F4_window_has_width
falsifiers.desc: PASS = the SC-leading window has finite interaction tolerance (>0.05 in U units), not a fragile knife-edge. FAIL = the window is a measure-zero coincidence.

falsifiers.name: F5_sdw_not_first_mover
falsifiers.desc: PASS = bare on-site SDW does not pre-empt SC. FAIL = flat-band magnetism (SDW) triggers first -> the canonical competing-order killer wins.
